config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu dianggap lebih penting, itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama “kebaikan bersama”. Di Indonesia, saya melihat pentingnya gotong royong dan kepentingan umum, tapi itu tidak boleh menghapus kebebasan dasar, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat tiap orang. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati, bukan dari pengorbanan paksa tanpa batas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily that idea can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require limits that affect everyone. The key is that society should justify those limits narrowly and transparently, rather than treating the person as disposable for some vague "greater good." In my view, a free society works best when individual rights are the default and collective needs are the exception, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kritis, membenarkan ketidakadilan, atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil yang paling rentan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi pembatasan hak harus sangat terbatas, jelas alasannya, dan tidak boleh merendahkan martabat orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru yang berani melindungi hak individu, bukan yang meminta individu terus-menerus berkorban tanpa batas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify racism, forced conformity, and government abuse. At the same time, I do not buy the extreme version of individualism that treats every personal choice as untouchable, because public health, safety, and shared infrastructure do require some limits. What matters to me is whether a restriction is narrowly tailored, accountable, and actually necessary, not whether it is wrapped in the language of the "greater good." A healthy society protects individual rights first and asks people to sacrifice only when the case is specific and convincing.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering jadi pembenaran untuk menekan yang lemah atau membungkam kritik. Memang benar ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik, bencana, atau ketertiban umum yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Saya juga tidak setuju dengan individualisme ekstrem yang mengabaikan gotong royong, karena hidup bersama memang butuh tanggung jawab sosial. Namun batasnya tetap harus hak dasar dan martabat manusia, sebab tanpa itu “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alat kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a United States perspective, I have seen how quickly “the common good” can become a cover for racism, censorship, forced conformity, or plain government abuse, so I am very skeptical of any claim that society should come first by default. That said, I also do not support absolute individualism, because public health, safety, and shared systems sometimes require real limits. The standard should be that any restriction on rights must be narrow, necessary, and accountable, with individual rights remaining the default rather than the thing society can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
